notes: Humayun. Babur (Founder of Mughal emperor) was descended from Timur and Genghis Khan. Mughal emperors chronologically : Babur → Humayun → Akbar → Jahangir → Shah-Jahan →Aurangzeb etc. Q.369. As per the Timurid tradition, Humayun had to share power with his brothers. Humayun had the control of Delhi, Agra and Central India, while his brother Kamran controlled which of the following regions? SSC CGL 19/09/2024 (1st Shift) (a) Afghanistan and Punjab (b) Deccan (c) Gujarat and Rajasthan (d) Bengal and Bihar Sol.369.(a) Afghanistan and Punjab. Kamran Mirza was the second son of Babur, the founder of the Mughal Empire. He was the brother of Humayun, who was born Nasir-ud-Din Muhammad in Kabul. Humayun was the second Emperor of the Mughal Empire, ruling over present-day Pakistan, Northern India, Afghanistan, and Bangladesh from 1530 to 1540 and again from 1555 to 1556.

Sher Shah Suri (Farid Khan, 1540-1545) - Founder of the Sur Empire, constructed the Grand Trunk Road, introduced the first Rupee (silver coin, Rupia). He defeated Humayun in 1539 and 1540 in the battle of Chausa and Kannauj respectively.

Iran. Sher Khan, an ethnic Pashtun, defeated Humayun in the battles of Chausa (1539) and battles of Bilgram/Kannauj (1540), forcing Humayun to flee to Iran. After these victories, Sher Khan crowned himself Farid al-Dīn Sher Shah and founded the Sur Dynasty.

Dinpanah. Humayun’s Contribution to Architecture: Humayun's Tomb (UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1993) - It was built by his widow Biga Begum (Hajji Begum), Purana Quila (Delhi) - Built as a part of the new city Dinpannah.

Bihar (Sasaram). Tombs of Mughal Emperors - Gardens of Babur (Kabul, Afghanistan), Humayun's Tomb (New Delhi), Akbar's Tomb (Sikandra, Uttar Pradesh), Tomb of Jahangir (Lahore, Pakistan), The Taj Mahal (Agra), Tomb of Aurangzeb (Ahmednagar). Q.374. Who among the following medieval rulers focused his attention on the construction of sarais (shelter) for the convenience of travellers ? SSC CHSL 10/08/2023 (4th shift) (a) Babur (b) Sher Shah Suri (c) Akbar (d) Humayun Sol.374.(b) Sher Shah Suri (1540-1545): Administrative reforms - Introduction of Rupiya, Built Grand-Trunk Road, Staged Dak-Chowkis in Sarais. Q.375. The historical Sher-Shah Suri Marg runs between ___________. SSC MTS 14/06/2023 (Morning) (a) Kanpur and Kanyakumari (b) Delhi and Manali (c) Delhi and Amritsar (d) Lucknow and Kolkata Sol.375.(c) Delhi and Amritsar. The Grand Trunk Road (formerly known as Uttarapath, Sarak-e-Azam and Badshahi Sarak) - Part of National Highway (12, 27, 19, 44, 3) in India.
Biga Begum. The architect was Mirak Mirza Ghiyath.
Humayun and son of Babur and Gulrukh Begum. He came to India in 1538.
